title: timonius fuscus
intro: Timonius fuscus, also known as the Asian rosewood or whitebacked Timonius, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and bark are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it is also used in traditional medicine to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and promote overall well-being.
